page: Microsoft Azure DS15_v2 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the DSv2 family, powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 20 dedicated physical cores and 20 vCPUs utilizing an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 140.0 GB of system memory, yielding 7.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside 287 GB of local storage. The processor is equipped with 48 MB of L3 cache and supports AVX, AVX512F, and AES instruction sets.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the DS15_v2 delivers average multi-core capabilities, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 25026.63 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 10945.0. Single-core performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng single-core scoring 1245.99 and single-threaded gzip compression reaching 20.76 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth is solid, measuring 1506.02 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 156.51 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Database operations and web serving are stable, with Redis SET operations handling 29.2 million operations per second and static web serving throughput reaching 26.14 GB/sec.

The primary tradeoff of this instance lies in its single-threaded processing and text generation speeds, which fall into the lower performance tier, making it less suitable for latency-sensitive single-core tasks or real-time LLM text generation. However, its balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated core allocation make it qualitatively cost-efficient for multi-threaded workloads. This server is best suited for general-purpose enterprise applications, relational databases, software compilation, and moderate-scale web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure DS15_v2 is a DSv2-family virtual machine configured with 20 dedicated vCPUs and 20 physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 140.0 GB of RAM (7.0 GB per core) and 287 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 instance delivers average multi-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench multi-core score of 10945.0 and a Redis SET throughput of 29.2 million operations per second. While single-threaded performance and LLM text generation speeds are relatively weak, its memory capacity and dedicated CPU allocation provide stable resource density. The DS15_v2 is well-suited for database operations, multi-threaded software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise workloads.
